name: OpenRouter API
what_it_does: Unified LLM gateway that routes requests to 200+ models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, and others through a single OpenAI-compatible API endpoint.
use_cases: Route agent LLM calls to the cheapest capable model for a given subtask, falling back to a more powerful model only when needed
Build model-agnostic agent frameworks that swap underlying LLMs without code changes by targeting OpenRouter's unified endpoint
Run parallel LLM requests to multiple models and compare outputs for ensemble or self-consistency techniques
Access models not available in your region or without direct API agreements (e.g., use Anthropic models without an Anthropic account)
Implement automatic failover when a primary model provider is experiencing an outage by using OpenRouter's provider routing fallback
not_for: Fine-tuning or training models — OpenRouter only provides inference routing, not model customization
Applications requiring guaranteed provider data privacy agreements — traffic passes through OpenRouter's infrastructure before reaching providers
Teams with existing high-volume direct provider contracts — direct provider pricing is often lower than OpenRouter markup at scale
best_when: You want model flexibility and fallback routing across multiple LLM providers through a single OpenAI-compatible integration point, especially for cost optimization or resilience.
avoid_when: You have a committed relationship with a single provider and need the lowest possible inference cost, or your compliance requirements prohibit third-party LLM routing intermediaries.

auth.notes: Single API key per account passed as Bearer token. Key can be provisioned with a credit limit for cost control. No per-model or per-endpoint scope granularity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $1 in free credits on signup; some models listed as free with rate limits
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-token at model-specific rates; OpenRouter adds a small markup over provider cost (typically 5-10%); no subscription required
pricing.notes: Prepay credits model — add credits to your account and consume them per request. Some free models are available with rate limits for testing. Markup varies by model.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits depend on the underlying model provider; OpenRouter itself does not publish hard global limits; per-model limits documented in model listings

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors follow OpenAI API error format for compatibility. Provider-level errors are sometimes surfaced with provider context, other times wrapped generically. Rate limit errors include provider attribution.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: LLM inference is inherently non-idempotent; no request deduplication. Agents must handle duplicate responses at the application layer if retrying failed requests.
known_agent_gotchas: Model availability fluctuates — a model listed as available may go offline without notice; agents should implement fallback model logic or use OpenRouter's provider fallback routing feature
Latency is higher than direct provider access due to routing overhead — latency-sensitive agent loops should benchmark OpenRouter vs direct provider carefully
Context window limits, supported parameters, and output formats vary per model — an agent prompt that works on GPT-4o may fail or truncate on a different model without parameter validation
OpenRouter does not support streaming SSE on all models consistently — test streaming behavior specifically for each model used in agent pipelines
Usage data and conversation content passes through OpenRouter servers before reaching the LLM provider — review OpenRouter's data processing terms before routing sensitive workloads

security_score_components.security_notes: No SOC2 or formal compliance certifications documented. Data passes through OpenRouter before reaching model providers — a concern for sensitive data. No fine-grained key scoping. Credit limits on keys reduce financial blast radius.
